headlineSummary: Overall sentiment is mixed-neutral: Gemini has limited evidence with one strong complaint and one compliment [E1][E2], while Anthropic and OpenAI are split by a close tie, with Anthropic showing slightly more balanced discussion [E5][E6][E7] [E3][E4].

ranking.summary: Gemini has limited evidence and reads mixed-neutral, with one sharp criticism of Gemini CLI alongside praise for a successful SVG-editing use case [E1][E2].

Anthropic is mixed-neutral with a close tie and medium support, combining praise for Claude Code’s useful site automation with complaints about Claude-generated messages and a mixed view of its consistency [E5][E6][E7].
OpenAI is mixed-neutral with limited evidence and a close tie, pairing praise for Codex handling a multi-step browser task with criticism of ChatGPT being used to polish messages [E3][E4].

evidence.excerpt: Google is so far behind agentic cli coding. Gemini CLI is awful. So bad in fact that it’s clear none of their team use it.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Gemini CLI and calls it "awful," adding that it is so bad the team seems not to use it.

evidence.excerpt: Favourite unexpected use case for me was telling gemini to use it as a SVG editing repl, where it was able to produce some fantastic looking custom icons for me after 3-4 generate/refresh/screenshot iterations.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Gemini produced "fantastic looking custom icons," which is clear praise of its output quality in this task.

evidence.excerpt: For example, I use codex to manage a local music library, and it was able to use the skill to open a YT Music tab in my browser, search for each album, and get the URL to pass to yt-dlp.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly credits Codex with successfully carrying out a useful multi-step browser task.

evidence.excerpt: Using ChatGPT to “polish” your message is not. If you’re not sure about your English abilities, we offer you free English lessons and encourage giving each other feedback during chats. LLMs shouldn’t be used for communication at all if you want any form of authenticity.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly rejects using ChatGPT for message polishing and says LLMs harm authenticity in communication.

evidence.excerpt: I use Playwright to intercept all requests and responses and have Claude Code navigate to a website like YouTube and click and interact with all the elements and inputs while recording all the requests and responses associated with each interaction. Then it creates a detailed strongly typed API to interact with any website using the underlying API.

annotations.rationale: The commenter describes Claude Code successfully navigating sites and generating a detailed typed API, indicating useful capability.

evidence.excerpt: This is starting to become my latest pet peeve, people using Claude to write their messages in Slack. I'm going to just stop communicating via text with these people. It's one thing to have Claude polish a message and another thing for it to write out an entire message.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly complains about people using Claude for Slack messages and reacts negatively to full message generation.

evidence.excerpt: The more I evaluate Claude Code, the more it feels like the world's most inconsistent golfer. It can get within a few paces of the hole in often a single strike, and then it'll spend hours, days, weeks trying to nail the putt.

annotations.rationale: This is mixed sentiment: Claude Code is credited with getting close quickly, but criticized as highly inconsistent and slow to finish tasks.
